title: Swipe Fatigue Test
hook: Quick gut check before you open the app again tonight 👇
slides: Quick gut check before you open the app again tonight 👇
How many people did you match with this week?
Now — how many did you actually meet in person?
If the second number is a lot smaller than the first... you're not alone. Matching isn't meeting.
KeepItUp skips the matching part. You just show up to a small, real game — a run, a rally, a climb — and meet whoever's actually there.
Open worldwide. Link in bio to join early. 🎾
caption: Be honest with yourself for one second: how many of this week's matches turned into an actual person in an actual room? We stopped trying to fix the matching and just skipped it — show up to a small real game and meet who's there. Open worldwide, link in bio.
cta: Save this for the next time you're about to open the app out of habit — comment ‘SAME’ if the gap between matches and meetups is bigger than you'd like to admit.
imageConcept: 6-card carousel, 4:5, anthracite (#20262B) background throughout. Card 1 (cover): bold off-white (#F1F5F3) headline text, neon-green (#3BEA7E) question mark motif in corner. Cards 2-4: one short line per card in large bold sans display type, a simple neon-green progress-style counter icon (e.g. a small bar/tally graphic) shrinking from card 2 to card 3 to visualize ‘matches vs meetups’ without using real numbers. Card 5: KeepItUp motion-arc mark appears small, top-left, as the tone shifts from problem to solution; icon of a small running-figure/racket silhouette in neon-green. Card 6 (CTA): KeepItUp wordmark centered, neon-green ‘link in bio’ arrow, off-white body text.

title: The Ick Files: Dating App Edition
trend: "the ick" relatable-confession format — our own on-brand riff on the theme, no borrowed audio or creator likeness
hook: POV: the ick isn't a person. It's the fifth ‘hey’ with no follow-up question.
script:
0:00-0:02 — Close-up of a phone screen (blurred/mocked chat bubbles), on-screen text: ‘the ick isn't a person.’ No VO yet, just a sigh sound.
0:02-0:05 — Person tosses phone onto the couch. On-screen text: ‘it's this 👇’ — cut to 3 quick mocked chat previews all reading ‘hey’ / ‘hey’ / ‘sup’.
0:05-0:09 — Fast montage of the same 3 mocked chats, on-screen counter text: ‘match #1... #2... #3...’ comedic repetition, upbeat cut rhythm.
0:09-0:13 — Cut to the same person now mid-laugh at a casual padel/run meetup, natural daylight, on-screen text: ‘vs. actually finding out if someone's fun to be around.’
0:13-0:18 — On-screen text, no talking: ‘no bio. no small-talk marathon. just show up and see.’
0:18-0:22 — End card: KeepItUp motion-arc mark on anthracite, on-screen text ‘meet through movement — open worldwide’, VO/caption-only line: ‘link's in bio if you're tired of hey too.’
caption: We did the math: the average ‘hey’ gets one reply and zero follow-up questions. So we stopped asking people to make small talk and started giving them something to actually do together. Open worldwide — link in bio.
cta: Comment your worst ‘hey’ opener — the funniest ones get a reply video.
imageConcept: Cover/thumbnail frame: anthracite background, bold neon-green text ‘the ick isn't a person’ over a blurred mocked-chat-bubble graphic (no real usernames/photos), KeepItUp mark small bottom-right corner, off-white subtext ‘it's the 5th hey.’

title: Which One Are You
hook: Tag which one you are before you scroll past 👇
caption: No judgment — we've all been at least one of these. Comment which one you are, and tag the friend who's definitely ‘The Ghost.’ (KeepItUp is our attempt to make more people end up as the third one.) Open worldwide, link in bio.
cta: Comment which one you are, then tag the friend who matches one of the other two.
imageConcept: Single 4:5 image, anthracite background, three vertical panel cards side by side. Panel 1 ‘The Swiper’: muted grey-on-anthracite text, small flat phone-silhouette icon, deliberately low-contrast/faded to feel tired. Panel 2 ‘The Ghost’: text rendered at reduced opacity with a fading trail effect, ghost-outline icon, muted grey tone. Panel 3 ‘The Rally Player’: full neon-green (#3BEA7E) highlight background within its card, bold off-white text, a simple racket/running-figure icon, clearly the visual ‘winner’ of the three without any text claiming superiority — the contrast alone tells the joke. KeepItUp wordmark small, centered bottom margin below all three panels.

title: Silent Rally Cam
trend: Ride the seasonal ‘viral wholesome sports moment’ theme per trend-jacking-playbook.md — theme only, no real athlete's name/likeness/footage
hook: Nobody said a word for 4 seconds. That's when you knew.
caption: Some of the best ‘getting to know you’ moments have zero dialogue — a good rally just does the talking. That's the whole bet behind KeepItUp: the activity carries the first encounter, not a script. Open worldwide, link in bio.
cta: Send this to the friend who always overthinks first-conversation small talk.
imageConcept: Carousel, brand system (anthracite #20262B, neon #3BEA7E, off-white text). Bold centered statement per slide; final slide flips to accent green for the CTA.
slides: Nobody said a word for 4 seconds. That’s when you knew. 👀
Some of the best ‘getting to know you’ moments have zero dialogue.
A good rally just does the talking — a look, a nod, a laugh after a great point.
No bio. No small-talk marathon. Just two people and a game.
That’s the whole bet behind KeepItUp: the activity carries the first encounter, not a script.
Open worldwide — link in bio to find your first game. 🎾

title: Rate My Date (Activity Edition)
trend: "rating things out of 10" rating-format trend, applied to first-date activities — no borrowed audio
hook: Rating first-date activities so you don't have to (as someone who has had... a lot)
caption: Rating first-date formats by how much they actually tell you about a person. Padel wins, obviously. Duet this with your own worst first-date idea and we'll rate it. Open worldwide, link in bio.
cta: Duet or stitch this with your own first-date rating — best ones get featured in a follow-up.
imageConcept: Carousel, brand system (anthracite #20262B, neon #3BEA7E, off-white text). Bold centered statement per slide; final slide flips to accent green for the CTA.
slides: Rating first-date ideas 1–10. Be honest. 👇
Dinner where you just stare and go ‘so… tell me about yourself.’ → 2/10. Interrogation energy.
Coffee that’s over in 25 minutes because you both ran out of questions. → 4/10. Fine. Forgettable.
A round of padel where you’re both bad and laughing about it. → 9/10. Actual chemistry check.
How someone reacts to losing a point tells you more than an hour of small talk ever will.
Open worldwide — link in bio to find your first game. 🎾

title: Green Flags You Can't Fake
hook: Every dating profile says ‘good communicator.’ Here's how to actually check.
slides: Every profile says ‘good communicator’ and ‘easygoing.’ Cool story. How would you actually know?
You can't fact-check a bio. You can watch someone for 90 minutes.
Do they encourage you when you're losing — or go quiet?
Do they wait up, or walk ahead?
Do they laugh when it goes sideways, or get weird about it?
None of that shows up in a chat thread. All of it shows up in a game.
That's why KeepItUp starts with the activity, not the profile. Open worldwide — link in bio.
caption: Green flags are easy to type and hard to prove. So we stopped asking people to describe themselves and started giving them something real to do together — the activity does the vetting. Open worldwide, link in bio.
cta: Save this for the next ‘good vibes only’ bio you read — then comment the sport you trust most to reveal someone's character.
imageConcept: 7-card carousel, 4:5, anthracite background throughout. Each card: one bold neon-green headline phrase plus off-white supporting text, consistent icon system — a crossed-out chat-bubble icon for the ‘can't fake’ cards, a running-figure/racket icon for the ‘shows up in a game’ cards. Final card: KeepItUp wordmark centered, neon-green ‘link in bio’ arrow.

title: Tag the Friend
hook: Tag the friend you'd drag to a padel court even though they've never played.
caption: Everyone has that one friend who'd actually come if you asked. Tag them. We'll see you both at the next one — open worldwide, link in bio.
cta: Native tag-a-friend sticker (IG Stories) / friend-tag comment prompt (TikTok) — track reach-from-tags and resulting profile-visit taps.
imageConcept: Vertical 9:16 story frame, anthracite background, bold neon-green centered headline ‘TAG THE FRIEND WHO'D ACTUALLY SHOW UP 🎾’, small arrow graphic pointing toward an empty reserved zone in the bottom third for the platform's native tag/mention sticker placement, KeepItUp mark small top corner.

title: Summer Loneliness Plot Twist
hook: It's the best weather of the entire year. Be honest — how much of it did you spend actually outside with people?
slides: It's the best weather of the entire year. Be honest — how much of it did you spend actually outside with people?
Summer is basically a 3-month permission slip to be outside. Most of us still spend a chunk of it scrolling indoors.
Not because we don't want people — because ‘let's get a drink sometime’ never quite turns into a plan.
So this summer, skip the maybe-drink. There's already a run, a rally, or a climb happening near you, with a time and a place set.
Show up. The activity does the rest.
Open worldwide. Link in bio — find the first one near you. ☀️🎾
caption: The sun's out. That ‘let's get a drink sometime’ text is still sitting unanswered. This summer, skip straight to the part where you actually show up — a run, a rally, a climb, already scheduled, already real. Open worldwide, link in bio.
cta: Comment the sport you wish you did more of this summer — we're using the replies to help decide what we open next.
imageConcept: 6-card carousel, 4:5, anthracite background with a warm sunlit highlight overlay (subtle warm-toned gradient wash, still anchored to the anthracite/neon-green/off-white system) and a small sun-arc motif echoing the brand's motion-arc mark. Each card: one short punchy line in bold sans display type. Final card: KeepItUp wordmark, neon-green ‘link in bio’ CTA, small sun icon.
